breaking.title: 6 killed as Russia rains missiles, drones on Ukraine; Kyiv renews plea for air defences
breaking.summary: Recent missile and drone attacks from Russia have claimed the lives of six individuals and left many others wounded throughout Ukraine. In response to dwindling interceptor missile availability, Kyiv has made an emergency appeal for air defense systems. The strikes affected key locations, including Sumy, Odesa, Kharkiv, and Kyiv, with significant casualties in Sumy itself. While much of the aerial offensive was intercepted, ballistic missiles remained unchallenged.

breaking.title: Ann Widdecombe attacked nearly 24 hours before she was found dead, police believe
breaking.summary: A 26-year-old white British man arrested on suspicion of her murder has been released and is no longer part of the investigation.

breaking.title: Trump threatens Iran with '1000 missiles' as both sides shoot their mouth in Gulf spat
breaking.summary: President Trump threatened Iran with massive missile strikes if assassination plots are pursued. This warning dramatically escalated tensions between the two nations. Disagreements over a maritime deal's interpretation fueled renewed military exchanges. Iran vowed revenge for past actions and perceived US treaty violations. Ambiguity and mistrust now threaten another fragile understanding between them.

breaking.title: Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
breaking.summary: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

breaking.title: China's second typhoon in a week makes landfall
breaking.summary: Nearly two million people are evacuated from eastern Zhejiang province, with the city of Wenzhou close to the path of the storm.

breaking.title: Iran must pledge to stop shooting at ships in Strait of Hormuz, US officials say
breaking.summary: US media say Iran has told US officials an attack on ships crossing the strait early this week was "a mistake".

breaking.title: ‘I’ll play for India’: Popular US streamer IShowSpeed makes bold offer to help nation qualify for 2030 FIFA World Cup
breaking.summary: IShowSpeed joked that he would play for India’s national football team and help the country qualify for the 2030 World Cup. The YouTuber made the comments during a livestream while watching Spain vs Belgium. He said he could play as a winger, striker or attacking midfielder. While FIFA rules make the idea unrealistic, his support drew attention from football fans.

breaking.title: Pakistan claims 88 militants killed in ongoing Balochistan crackdown
breaking.summary: Pakistan's security forces have killed eighty-eight militants in Balochistan since July fifth. Operation Shaban involves the army and paramilitary forces targeting militant hideouts. This operation began after coordinated attacks rocked the province on July fifth. Militants killed nine police personnel and abducted eighteen others at a checkpoint. Violence also struck a local tribe, killing five and abducting eleven individuals.
